{"key_event":"Post-9/11 security-state expansion","reasoning":"The PATRIOT Act, DHS/TSA, intelligence growth, war powers, detention, and surveillance pushed an already very powerful federal state higher, though Katrina and financial-regulatory failures showed limits to competence. Civil society remained strong through press exposure, courts, antiwar protest, and online mobilization, but polarization, labor decline, civil-liberties erosion, and the financial crisis weakened its cohesive checking capacity.","society_change":-0.2,"society_power":8.1,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":9.4}
